name: Paladin
description: Paladins are united by their oaths to stand against the forces of annihilation and corruption. Whether sworn before a god's altar, in a sacred glade before nature spirits and Fey witnesses, or in a moment of desperation and grief with the dead as the only witnesses, a Paladin's oath is a powerful bond. It is a source of power that turns a devout warrior into a blessed champion. A Paladin swears to stand against corrupting influences and to hunt the forces of rampant destruction wherever they lurk. Different Paladins focus on various aspects of these causes, but all are bound by the oaths that grant them power to do their sacred work. Although many Paladins are devoted to gods of good, a Paladin's power comes from a commitment to justice itself, not from a god.

features.name: Lay on Hands
features.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a Long Rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of Hit Points equal to your Paladin level times 5. As a Bonus Action, you can touch a creature (which can be yourself) and draw power from the pool to restore a number of Hit Points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in your pool. You can also expend 5 Hit Points from your pool of healing to remove the Poisoned condition from the creature; this doesn't restore Hit Points to the creature.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Longswords and Javelins. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the kinds of weapons you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of Halberds and Flails.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Instead of choosing one of those feats, you can choose the option below: Blessed Warrior: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Cleric spell list. The cantrips count as Paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip from the Cleric spell list.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You have learned to cast spells through prayer and meditation. You can prepare two level 1 spells of your choice from the Paladin spell list. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your Paladin spells.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain the ability to channel divine energy to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Divine Sense and Turn Undead. You can use Channel Divinity once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Short or Long Rest. Some Channel Divinity effects require saving throws; the save DC equals 8 plus your Proficiency Bonus plus your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Paladin Subclass
features.description: You gain a Paladin subclass of your choice. A subclass represents the oath you swore and grants you features at 3rd level and again at 7th, 15th, and 20th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Faithful Steed
features.description: You can call on the aid of an otherworldly steed. You can cast the Find Steed spell without expending a spell slot. You can also cast it using a level 2+ spell slot as normal. Once you cast the spell without expending a spell slot, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: You radiate a protective aura while you aren't Incapacitated. You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you have a bonus to saving throws equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum bonus of +1). If another Paladin is present, a creature can benefit from only one Aura of Protection at a time (the creature chooses which one while in the overlapping auras).

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Abjure Foes
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to overwhelm your foes with awe. As you present your Holy Symbol or Druidcraft Focus, each creature of your choice within 60 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw if it can see you. On a failed save, the creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute or until it takes any damage. While Frightened in this way, the creature has the Restrained condition.

features.name: Aura of Courage
features.description: You and your allies are bolstered against fear. You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you are immune to the Frightened condition while you aren't Incapacitated. If another Paladin is present, a creature can benefit from only one Aura of Courage at a time (the creature chooses which one while in the overlapping auras).

features.name: Radiant Strikes
features.description: Your strikes now carry supernatural power. When you hit a target with a Melee weapon or an Unarmed Strike, the target takes an extra 1d8 Radiant damage.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Abjure Foes Improvement
features.description: When you use your Abjure Foes feature, you can target a number of creatures equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature).

features.name: Restoring Touch
features.description: When you use Lay on Hands on a creature, you can also remove one or more of the following conditions from the creature: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, or Stunned. You must expend 5 Hit Points from the healing pool for each of these conditions you remove; this expenditure is in addition to the Hit Points you might also restore.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Aura Improvements
features.description: Your auras now affect friendly creatures within 30 feet of you rather than 10 feet. If another Paladin is present, a creature still can benefit from only one aura of each type at a time.

features.name: Channel Divinity Improvement
features.description: You can now use your Channel Divinity twice between rests.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

subclasses.name: Oath of Devotion
subclasses.description: The Oath of Devotion binds Paladins to the loftiest ideals of justice, virtue, and order. Sometimes called Cavaliers, White Knights, or Holy Warriors, these Paladins meet the ideal of the knight in shining armor, acting with honor in pursuit of justice and the greater good. They hold themselves to the highest standards of conduct, and some, for better or worse, hold the rest of the world to the same standards.
tenets: Honesty: Don't lie or cheat. Let your word be your promise.
Courage: Never fear to act, though caution is wise.
Compassion: Aid others, protect the weak, and punish those who threaten them. Show mercy to your foes, but temper it with wisdom.
Honor: Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example to them. Do as much good as possible while causing the least amount of harm.
Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you.

3.name: Sacred Weapon
3.description: As a Bonus Action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1). The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. If the weapon is not already magical, it becomes magical for the duration. You can end this effect as a Bonus Action.

7.name: Aura of Devotion
7.description: You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you are immune to the Charmed condition while you aren't Incapacitated. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

15.name: Smite of Protection
15.description: When you use your Divine Smite feature, you regain Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt by the smite.

20.name: Sacred Nimbus
20.description: As a Bonus Action, you can imbue yourself with a Sacred Nimbus for 1 minute. The nimbus sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. Whenever an enemy creature starts its turn in the Bright Light, the creature takes 10 Radiant damage. In addition, for the duration, you have Advantage on saving throws, and other creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
